sections.heading: About this Task
sections.body:
*Estimated completion time: 5 minutes*

Use this tutorial to load sample data into your Atlas
clusters. You can load sample data into your Atlas
cluster from the Atlas CLI or the [Atlas UI](atlas-ui). Select your interface from the drop-down menu.

sections.heading: Before You Begin
sections.body:
To utilize the sample data provided by Atlas, you must create
an Atlas cluster to load data into. To learn more,
see [Cluster Types](create-new-database-deployment).

To load sample data, you must also have
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project.

Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves to the
project as a `Project Owner`.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.body:
<SelectedContent>
  **Clusters-view:**

  Clusters View

  To load sample data into your cluster from
  the **Clusters** view:

  # Step 1: Open the  dialog box.

  - Locate the cluster where you want to load
    sample data.

  - Click the Ellipses (...) button for your
    cluster.

  - Click Load Sample Dataset.

    The Load sample data dialog box opens.

  - In the dialog box, choose which datasets to load from the drop-down menu.

    For details on the collections and documents included in these
    datasets, see [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets).

    To load all available sample datasets, click Select All.

  # Step 2: Click  to confirm.

  The dialog box closes and Atlas begins loading your sample
  dataset into your cluster.

  # Step 3: View your sample data.

  You should see the databases that you loaded in your
  cluster in the Collections view.

  # Step 4: (Optional) Run a query on the sample data.

  For example, if you loaded the `sample_restaurants` dataset:

  - In the left navigation of the Collections page, select
    the `sample_restaurants` database and then the `restaurants` collection.

  - To find all restaurants located in Queens, copy the following
    [query filter document](manual/core/document) into the Filter search bar.

    ```json
    { borough: "Queens" }
    ```

  - Click Apply.

  - Atlas shows documents where the `borough` field corresponds to `Queens`.

    To learn more, see [Query Documents](manual/tutorial/query-documents).

  **Collections-view:**

  Collections View

  > **Important:** You can load sample data through the
  > **Collection View** only if you have
  > **Data Explorer** [enabled](disable-data-explorer) and you don't have
  > any data already in your collection.

  To load sample data into your cluster from the **Collections View**:

  # Step 1: Click .

  The Load sample data dialog box opens.

  # Step 2: In the dialog box, select which datasets to load from the drop-down menu.

  For details on the collections and documents included in these
  datasets, see [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets).

  To load all available sample datasets, click Select All.

  # Step 3: Click  to confirm.

  Once the load completes, the view refreshes to show your sample
  data.

  You should see the databases that you loaded in your
  cluster.

  # Step 4: (Optional) Run a query on the sample data.

  For example, if you loaded the `sample_restaurants` dataset:

  - In the left navigation of the Collections page, select
    the `sample_restaurants` database and then the `restaurants` collection.

  - To find all restaurants located in Queens, copy the following
    [query filter document](manual/core/document) into the Filter search bar.

    ```json
    { borough: "Queens" }
    ```

  - Click Apply.

  - Atlas shows documents where the `borough` field is equal to `Queens`.

    To learn more, see [Query Documents](manual/tutorial/query-documents).
</SelectedContent>To load sample data into the specified cluster using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas clusters sampleData load <clusterName> [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas clusters sampleData load](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-sampleData-load).After you run the command to load sample data, you can use
the following Atlas CLI commands to monitor the status of the
sample data load job:To return the details for the specified sample data load job using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas clusters sampleData describe <id> [options]

```To watch the specified sample data job in your cluster until it completes using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas clusters sampleData watch <id> [options]

```To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas clusters sampleData describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-sampleData-describe) and
[atlas clusters sampleData watch](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-sampleData-watch).Related Links- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

sections.heading: Learn More
sections.body:
You can also generate synthetic data that aligns to your real data's
schema. To learn more, see [Generate Synthetic Data](synthetic-data).

To import your own data, see [Migrate or Import Data](import-strategies).
